title.en: Transformer Architecture: Attention Is All You Need
title.ta: டிரான்ஸ்ஃபார்மர் கட்டமைப்பு: கவனம்தான் உங்களுக்கு தேவையான அனைத்தும்

description.en: Build a Transformer from scratch with encoder-decoder structure, multi-head attention, positional encoding, and feed-forward networks.
description.ta: என்கோடர்-டிகோடர் அமைப்பு, multi-head attention, positional encoding, feed-forward networks உடன் scratch-லிருந்து Transformer உருவாக்குங்கள்.

story_variants.en:
## Vaathiyaar's Translation Machine

Vaathiyaar stands before a machine with two halves. "The left half (encoder) reads the entire input at once. The right half (decoder) generates the output one token at a time, peeking at the encoder's work. This is the Transformer — the architecture that powers GPT, BERT, and nearly all modern AI."

### Transformer Components

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import math

class PositionalEncoding(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_model, max_len=5000):
        super().__init__()
        pe = torch.zeros(max_len, d_model)
        position = torch.arange(0, max_len).unsqueeze(1).float()
        div_term = torch.exp(torch.arange(0, d_model, 2).float() * -(math.log(10000.0) / d_model))
        pe[:, 0::2] = torch.sin(position * div_term)
        pe[:, 1::2] = torch.cos(position * div_term)
        self.register_buffer('pe', pe.unsqueeze(0))
    
    def forward(self, x):
        return x + self.pe[:, :x.size(1)]

class TransformerBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_model, num_heads, d_ff, dropout=0.1):
        super().__init__()
        self.attention = nn.MultiheadAttention(d_model, num_heads, batch_first=True)
        self.norm1 = nn.LayerNorm(d_model)
        self.ff = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(d_model, d_ff),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(d_ff, d_model)
        )
        self.norm2 = nn.LayerNorm(d_model)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout)
    
    def forward(self, x, mask=None):
        # Self-attention + residual + layer norm
        attn_out, _ = self.attention(x, x, x, attn_mask=mask)
        x = self.norm1(x + self.dropout(attn_out))
        # Feed-forward + residual + layer norm
        ff_out = self.ff(x)
        x = self.norm2(x + self.dropout(ff_out))
        return x

class MiniTransformer(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, vocab_size, d_model=128, num_heads=8, num_layers=4, d_ff=512):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, d_model)
        self.pos_encoding = PositionalEncoding(d_model)
        self.layers = nn.ModuleList([
            TransformerBlock(d_model, num_heads, d_ff) for _ in range(num_layers)
        ])
        self.fc_out = nn.Linear(d_model, vocab_size)
        self.d_model = d_model
    
    def forward(self, x, mask=None):
        x = self.embedding(x) * math.sqrt(self.d_model)
        x = self.pos_encoding(x)
        for layer in self.layers:
            x = layer(x, mask)
        return self.fc_out(x)

model = MiniTransformer(vocab_size=10000)
print(f'Parameters: {sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters()):,}')
```

> **Why Transformers Beat RNNs:** No sequential processing — all tokens processed in parallel. Attention connects any two tokens directly, regardless of distance!
story_variants.ta:
## வாத்தியாரின் மொழிபெயர்ப்பு இயந்திரம்

வாத்தியார் இரண்டு பாதிகள் கொண்ட இயந்திரத்தின் முன் நிற்கிறார். "இடது பாதி (encoder) முழு உள்ளீட்டையும் ஒரே நேரத்தில் படிக்கிறது. வலது பாதி (decoder) ஒரு நேரத்தில் ஒரு token-ஐ உருவாக்குகிறது. இது Transformer — GPT, BERT மற்றும் நவீன AI-ஐ இயக்கும் கட்டமைப்பு."

explanation.en: Positional encoding: sine waves of different frequencies encode token position. Even indices use sin
Odd indices use cos. This gives unique position-dependent patterns the model can learn from
Multi-head self-attention: 8 parallel attention heads each learning different patterns
Layer normalization: normalizes across features (not batch). Stabilizes transformer training
Self-attention: Q=K=V=x. Optional mask for causal (decoder) or padding masking
Residual connection (x + attn_out) followed by layer norm. Same as ResNet idea!
Feed-forward network: expand to 4x dimension then compress back. d_ff is typically 4*d_model
Second residual + norm. Each transformer block: attention+residual+norm, then ff+residual+norm
Scale embeddings by sqrt(d_model) to match positional encoding magnitude

steps.line: for layer in self.layers: x = layer(x, mask)

explanation.en: Stack N transformer blocks. Each refines representations. GPT-3 uses 96 layers!

left.label: RNN/LSTM
left.content:
Sequential processing
O(n) steps for n tokens
Vanishing gradients
Hard to parallelize

right.label: Transformer
right.content:
Parallel processing
O(1) attention connects any two tokens
No vanishing gradients
Highly parallelizable on GPUs

instruction.en: Build a mini-Transformer language model and train it to predict the next token on a small text corpus. Generate text by sampling from the trained model.
instruction.ta: ஒரு சிறிய உரை தொகுப்பில் அடுத்த token-ஐ கணிக்க பயிற்சி செய்யும் mini-Transformer மொழி மாதிரியை உருவாக்குங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import math

# Simple tokenized corpus (word-level)
text = 'the cat sat on the mat the dog ran in the park the bird flew over the tree'
tokens = text.split()
vocab = {w: i for i, w in enumerate(sorted(set(tokens)))}
idx_to_word = {i: w for w, i in vocab.items()}
encoded = torch.tensor([vocab[t] for t in tokens])

# TODO: Create input-target pairs (predict next word)
# TODO: Build MiniTransformer with causal mask
# TODO: Train for 200 epochs
# TODO: Generate text starting from 'the'
practice_challenges.expected_output: Generated: the cat sat on the mat the dog ran...

en: Create causal mask: torch.triu(torch.ones(seq_len, seq_len), diagonal=1).bool()
Use CrossEntropyLoss with output reshaped to (seq_len, vocab_size)
For generation, feed tokens one at a time with the mask

q.en: Why do Transformers need positional encoding?

en: Self-attention has no notion of order; positional encoding adds position information
ta: Self-attention-க்கு வரிசை பற்றிய எண்ணம் இல்லை; positional encoding நிலை தகவலைச் சேர்க்கிறது

q.en: What is the purpose of the feed-forward network in each Transformer block?
q.ta: ஒவ்வொரு Transformer block-லும் feed-forward network-ன் நோக்கம் என்ன?

en: To add non-linear transformation and increase model capacity per position
ta: ஒவ்வொரு நிலையிலும் non-linear transformation மற்றும் மாதிரி திறனை அதிகரிக்க

q.en: What is a causal mask in a decoder Transformer?

en: It prevents each position from attending to future positions
ta: ஒவ்வொரு நிலையும் எதிர்கால நிலைகளுக்கு கவனம் செலுத்துவதைத் தடுக்கிறது
